name: Baton Process Manager
description: Process management for Baton. Provides functionality to start, stop, and track service subprocesses for nodes. Manages subprocess lifecycle with graceful termination (SIGTERM followed by SIGKILL on timeout).

types.name: ProcessInfo
types.description: Tracked process information for a managed subprocess.

types.name: ProcessManager

fields.name: _processes
fields.description: Internal process tracking dictionary keyed by node_name

types.description: Manages service subprocesses with tracking by node name. Maintains internal dictionary of ProcessInfo keyed by node_name.

functions.name: __init__
functions.description: Initialize a new ProcessManager with an empty process tracking dictionary.
postconditions: self._processes is initialized as empty dict[str, ProcessInfo]

functions.name: processes
functions.description: Property getter that returns a copy of the tracked processes dictionary.
postconditions: Returns a new dict containing current processes
Returned dict is a copy, not a reference to internal state

functions.name: start
functions.description: Start a subprocess for a node using shell command. If a process for the node_name already exists, it is stopped first. Creates subprocess with piped stdout/stderr and optional environment variables.
preconditions: command is a valid shell command string
postconditions: ProcessInfo is returned with valid pid and process object
Log entry is written for process start

structured_side_effects.description: Adds or replaces ProcessInfo for node_name
Logs process start with pid and command
Creates and starts subprocess via asyncio.create_subprocess_shell

functions.name: stop
functions.description: Stop a subprocess gracefully by sending SIGTERM. If process does not exit within timeout, sends SIGKILL. Removes the process from tracking. Does nothing if node_name is not found or process already exited.

inputs.name: node_name
inputs.description: Node name whose process to stop

error_cases.name: process_lookup_error
error_cases.condition: Process already gone when trying to terminate

postconditions: Process is terminated and removed from self._processes
If process exists and is running, SIGTERM is sent
If timeout expires, SIGKILL is sent
Log entry is written for process stop

structured_side_effects.description: Logs process stop and potential SIGKILL warning

functions.name: stop_all
functions.description: Stop all tracked processes by calling stop() for each node_name in the current process dictionary.

inputs.name: timeout
inputs.description: Timeout passed to each stop() call

postconditions: All processes in self._processes are stopped

functions.name: is_running
functions.description: Check if a process for the given node_name is tracked and still running (returncode is None).
postconditions: Returns True if process exists and returncode is None
Returns False if process not found or has exited

functions.name: get_pid
functions.description: Get the PID for a node's tracked process. Returns None if process is not found.

inputs.name: node_name
inputs.description: Node name whose PID to retrieve

postconditions: Returns int PID if process is tracked
Returns None if node_name not found

invariants: Each node_name maps to at most one ProcessInfo in _processes
_processes keys are always valid node_name strings
ProcessInfo.node_name matches the key in _processes dictionary
